title: AD Taylor-tower P10: checkpointed high-order reverse with tower-valued tape rematerialization
goal: Per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md Frontier P10. Add memory-efficient reverse-over-Taylor using Griewank binomial checkpointing/rematerialization adapted to tower-valued primals and adjoints. Store checkpoints containing primal inputs, epoch tag, order K, coefficient type, and AOT-stable replay metadata; rematerialize forward tape segments during the reverse sweep under the same FP-contraction policy so replayed tower coefficients match the original pass. The pure-Scheme tape lib/core/ad/tape.esk must use explicit records/counting loops and respect AOT constraints (no unsupported higher-order iteration in core modules).
acceptance: high-order reverse oracle on a deep scalar graph PASS to d=8 within a fixed memory budget that naive O(tape*K) storage would exceed
checkpointed gradients agree with dense-tape baseline and analytic references
max tape bytes, checkpoint count, and rematerialization count are recorded in the oracle output
impure or nondeterministic segments conservatively fall back to dense tape or fail the budget explicitly
icc ad_depth and memory-budget criteria PASS
campaign_payoff: Makes high-order reverse practical for deep graphs by removing the O(tape*K) memory blowup while preserving local AOT verification.
